steps.task: Scan the codebase for security vulnerabilities. Focus on auth, input validation, and secrets exposure. Report findings.
Based on the security scan results, attempt to exploit the top 3 findings. Prove they are real vulnerabilities.
Review the vulnerability report and exploitation results. Prioritize fixes by severity and effort.
Generate a security audit report summarizing all findings, exploitation results, and recommended fixes with priority.
Analyze the feature requirements and design the architecture. Define component boundaries and APIs.
Write comprehensive tests for the designed architecture. Cover happy paths and edge cases.
Verify the CI/CD pipeline is configured for the new components. Update deployment configs if needed.
Create a clean commit with descriptive message and push to the feature branch.
Identify tech debt: deprecated patterns, dead code, unnecessary complexity. List everything that should be removed.
Review the tech debt findings. For each item, assess impact and suggest a concrete refactoring approach.
Check test coverage for the areas identified as tech debt. Write missing tests before refactoring.
Generate a Code Health Report with findings, priorities, estimated effort, and recommended sprint allocation.
Find edge cases, race conditions, and failure modes. Test with null, undefined, NaN, empty strings, huge inputs.
Write regression tests for every edge case found by the chaos engineer.
Review the chaos findings and tests. Ensure all critical paths are hardened.
Check the current state of the repo: recent commits, open PRs, failing CI, pending reviews.
Generate a daily standup report: what was done yesterday, what's planned today, any blockers.

morning-standup.description: Run daily standup every weekday at 8am

weekly-health.description: Code health check every Monday at 9am
